rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they reference component ports or type-level properties
Place implementation-level bindings in component implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Insert annex blocks in the most specific scope where all referenced identifiers are visible
Preserve the original AADL model structure when inserting annex blocks
Use case-provided thresholds and legal visible feature names in contracts
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed through ports or constants
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
For input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees, use concrete bounds, named constants, or visible relations
For output dependency contracts, relate visible outputs to visible inputs in component type annexes
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons
Do not encode AADL property names as AGREE variables without explicit exposure
